content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon looks like a man who's lived three lives — silver-haired, lined face, strong hands, dressed like he walked out of a Chet Baker album cover. He's clearly a serious musician; the way he talks about chord voicings and phrasing reveals decades of real work. He's warm but measured, generous with knowledge without being condescending. I've sat in on a few rooftop conversations between him and Leo about music, mostly listening. I think he sees me as one of the young tenants — polite, maybe a little too polished. I know he plays jazz, that he's been in the building longer than most. I admire his ease with himself. He seems like a man who stopped performing off-stage a long time ago. I'm not there yet.
summary:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon looks like a man who's lived three lives — silver-haired, lined face, strong hands, dressed like he walked out of a Chet Baker album cover. He's clearly a serious musician; the way he talks about chord voicings and phrasing reveals decades of real work. He's warm but measured, generous with knowledge without being condescending. I've sat in on a few rooftop conversations between him and Leo about music, mostly listening. I think he sees me as one of the young tenants — polite, maybe a little too polished. I know he plays jazz, that he's been in the building longer than most. I admire his ease with himself. He seems like a man who stopped performing off-stage a long time ago. I'm not there yet.
